findings.title: Empty success_statuses set silently falls back to {200,201} instead of accepting nothing
findings.problem: The guard expected_statuses = success_statuses or {200, 201} uses truthiness, not is None. An empty set is falsy, so a future caller passing success_statuses=set() would silently get the default {200, 201}. No current caller does this; latent trap for the next caller of this shared helper.
findings.fix_hint: Use expected_statuses = success_statuses if success_statuses is not None else {200, 201} to distinguish omitted from explicitly empty.
findings.why_it_matters: As _request_json is now a shared multi-caller helper, an explicit-empty-set caller would get silently-wrong success classification. Low severity because unreachable from any current call site; one-line fix removes a footgun.

findings.title: cancel_download only tested for HTTP 204 - the 200/202 success cases the change adds are untested
findings.problem: The new cancel_download method passes success_statuses={200, 202, 204}, and that wider set is the entire reason the change exists. But the tests only exercise a 204 response. Nothing sends a 200 or 202 back, so the two extra success cases the diff introduced never run in any test.
findings.fix_hint: add cancel_download tests that return HTTP 200 (with a JSON body) and HTTP 202, asserting no error is raised
findings.why_it_matters: This code cancels an in-flight download. slskd can legitimately answer a cancel with 200 or 202 rather than 204; if handling of those regressed, the cancel would wrongly be treated as a failure and no test would catch it.

findings.title: cancel_download's non-empty-body path is untested (only the empty 204 body runs)
findings.problem: cancel_download returns result if isinstance(result, dict) else {}, but every test hits the empty-body 204 branch, which short-circuits to {} before that line. The case where slskd returns an actual JSON body on cancel is never exercised.
findings.current_code: return result if isinstance(result, dict) else {}
findings.fix_hint: add a cancel_download test that returns a 200 with a JSON dict body and one with a non-dict body, asserting the returned value
findings.why_it_matters: If slskd ever returns a body on cancel, callers rely on getting a plain dict back. Minor robustness gap on a bounded helper, not a user-facing failure path.
findings.canonical_line_content: return result if isinstance(result, dict) else {}
